verses.flow: For He will give His angels charge concerning you, to guard you in all your ways.
verses.note: CHRIST TYPOLOGY: "For He will give His angels charge concerning you, to guard you in all your ways" — Satan quotes this verse (and v.12) to Jesus during the temptation (Matt. 4:6; Luke 4:10-11), omitting "in all your ways" to twist the promise into a justification for presumption. Jesus responds with Deut. 6:16: "You shall not put the LORD your God to the test." The psalm's promise is real, but Satan's application was a perversion — demanding miraculous protection rather than trusting in God's sovereign care.

verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: "The LORD reigns" (YHWH malakh) — this declaration opens the enthronement psalms (93, 96-99). The Hebrew can also be translated "The LORD has become king" or "The LORD is king." The affirmation that YHWH reigns is set against the backdrop of chaos (the floods, v.3) that threatens to overwhelm creation. God's kingship is established precisely in His victory over chaos.

verses.flow: For He is our God, and we are the people of His pasture and the sheep of His hand. Today, if you would hear His voice,
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL / CHRIST TYPOLOGY: "Today, if you would hear His voice, do not harden your hearts" — this is one of the most extensively quoted OT passages in the NT. Hebrews 3:7-11 attributes these words directly to the Holy Spirit and applies them to the church. Heb. 3:13, 15; 4:7 repeat "Today" as an urgent call. The warning is against the same unbelief that prevented the Exodus generation from entering the promised land. The author of Hebrews argues that a "rest" (katapausis, corresponding to menuchah) remains for God's people, and "today" is always the day to respond in faith.

verses.flow: do not harden your hearts, as at Meribah, as in the day of Massah in the wilderness,
verses.note: HEBREW: "Meribah" (merivah = "strife, contention") and "Massah" (massah = "testing, trial") — the place names encode Israel's failure (Exod. 17:1-7). They tested God despite having seen His works for forty years (v.10). The lesson: witnessing miracles does not guarantee faithfulness; only a responsive heart does.

verses.note: HEBREW: "All the gods of the peoples are idols" (kol 'elohey ha'ammim 'elilim) — a devastating wordplay. The gods (elohim) of the nations are 'elilim (worthless things, nothings, idols). The word 'elilim sounds like elohim but means the opposite. The true God made the heavens (shamayim); the false gods are nothing.

verses.flow: Know that the LORD Himself is God; it is He who has made us, and not we ourselves; we are His people and the sheep of His pasture.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: "Know that the LORD Himself is God; it is He who has made us, and not we ourselves" (de'u ki-YHWH hu' Elohim, hu' 'asanu welo' 'anachnu) — a fundamental creedal statement. The Qere (reading tradition) has lo' with aleph ("not"), making it "He made us and not we ourselves." The Ketiv (written text) has lo with vav ("His"), yielding "He made us and we are His." Both readings are theologically true and complementary. We are creatures, not self-made; we belong to God.

verses.flow: Enter His gates with thanksgiving and His courts with praise. Give thanks to Him, bless His name.
For the LORD is good; His lovingkindness is everlasting and His faithfulness to all generations.
A Psalm of David. I will sing of lovingkindness and justice; to You, O LORD, I will sing praises.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: A royal psalm of commitment — David's "oath of office." The king pledges personal integrity (v.2), purity of vision (v.3), separation from evil (vv.4-5), and righteous governance (vv.6-8). This is the ideal of Davidic kingship that no human king fully achieved, pointing forward to Christ the righteous King.

verses.flow: I will give heed to the blameless way. When will You come to me? I will walk within my house in the integrity of my heart.
I will set no worthless thing before my eyes; I hate the work of those who fall away; it shall not cling to me.
A perverse heart shall depart from me; I will know no evil.
Whoever secretly slanders his neighbor, him I will destroy; no one who has a haughty look and an arrogant heart will I endure.

verses.flow: I say, "O my God, do not take me away in the midst of my days, Your years are throughout all generations.
verses.note: CHRIST TYPOLOGY: "Of old You founded the earth, and the heavens are the work of Your hands" — Hebrews 1:10-12 quotes vv.25-27 and applies them directly to Christ, the Son. The author of Hebrews takes a psalm addressed to YHWH and attributes its words to the Son, demonstrating that the Son is the Creator who endures when all creation perishes. This is one of the strongest NT affirmations of Christ's deity and eternality.

verses.flow: Even they will perish, but You endure; and all of them will wear out like a garment; like clothing You will change them and they will be changed.
verses.note: HEBREW: "Like clothing You will change them and they will be changed" (kallevush tachalifem weyachalofu) — the heavens and earth are likened to worn-out garments that God will replace. "But You are the same" (we'attah hu') — the pronoun hu' ("He," here "the same") is almost a divine name, expressing God's unchanging identity. This is applied to Christ in Heb. 1:12 and 13:8: "Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever."

verses.flow: But You are the same, and Your years will not come to an end.
The children of Your servants will continue, and their descendants will be established before You."
Of David. Bless the LORD, O my soul, and all that is within me, bless His holy name.
verses.note: HEBREW: "Bless the LORD, O my soul" (barekhi nafshi 'et-YHWH) — the psalm opens and closes with this summons (vv.1-2, 22). Unlike most psalms that ask God to bless the worshiper, here the worshiper blesses God. Barakh means to kneel in homage, to speak well of, to praise. The soul (nephesh) and "all that is within me" (kol qeravai) indicate total, interior worship.

The LORD is compassionate and gracious, slow to anger and abounding in lovingkindness.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: "The LORD is compassionate and gracious, slow to anger and abounding in lovingkindness" (rachum wechannun YHWH, 'erekh 'appayim werav-chesed) — this is the great self-disclosure formula from Exod. 34:6-7, the most frequently quoted creedal statement in the Old Testament. It appears in Neh. 9:17; Joel 2:13; Jonah 4:2; Nah. 1:3; Pss. 86:15; 145:8. It is the closest thing to a systematic theology of God's character in the Hebrew Bible.

verses.flow: He has not dealt with us according to our sins, nor rewarded us according to our iniquities.
For as high as the heavens are above the earth, so great is His lovingkindness toward those who fear Him.
As far as the east is from the west, so far has He removed our transgressions from us.
verses.note: HEBREW: "As far as the east is from the west" (kirchoq mizrach mimma'arav) — an immeasurable distance, since east and west never meet. This is the psalm's supreme image of forgiveness: our sins are placed at infinite remove from us. Unlike north and south, which have poles, east and west are perpetually divergent.

verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: "Covering Yourself with light as with a cloak" ('oteh-'or kassalmah) — God's first act of self-revelation is light (Gen. 1:3). The psalm follows the creation order of Genesis 1: light (v.2), firmament/waters (vv.2-4), dry land (vv.5-9), vegetation (vv.14-16), luminaries (v.19), sea creatures (v.25-26), provision (vv.27-28). This is the poetic counterpart to the Genesis 1 narrative.

verses.flow: He lays the beams of His upper chambers in the waters; He makes the clouds His chariot; He walks upon the wings of the wind;

verses.note: HEBREW: "He makes the winds His messengers, flaming fire His ministers" ('oseh mal'akhav ruchot mesharetav 'esh lohet) — Heb. 1:7 quotes this to contrast angels (who are created servants) with the Son (who is eternal Creator, vv.25-27 via Ps. 102). Mal'akh means both "messenger" and "angel." Ruach means both "wind" and "spirit." The ambiguity is theologically rich.

verses.note: HEBREW: "Leviathan, which You have formed to sport in it" (Livyatan zeh yatsarta lesacheq-bo) — Leviathan appears here not as a chaos monster to be defeated (as in Ps. 74:14; Isa. 27:1) but as God's plaything, created for sport. The sea, which terrified ancient peoples, is merely God's toy box. This is one of the most audacious statements of divine sovereignty in Scripture (cf. Job 41).

He has remembered His covenant forever, the word which He commanded to a thousand generations,
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: "He has remembered His covenant forever" (zakhar le'olam berito) — the verb zakhar (remember) does not mean God forgot; it means God acts on the basis of His covenant commitment. The psalm traces God's faithfulness from Abraham (v.9) through Isaac (v.9), Jacob (v.10), the patriarchal sojourning (vv.12-15), Joseph (vv.16-22), Egypt (vv.23-38), the wilderness (vv.39-41), to the land (vv.44-45). Every event serves the covenant.

"They exchanged their glory for the image of an ox" (v.20) — Paul echoes this language in Rom. 1:23 to describe humanity's universal idolatry: they "exchanged the glory of the incorruptible God for an image." The pattern of exchanging truth for a lie is the fundamental human sin.

verses.flow: With my mouth I will give thanks abundantly to the LORD; and in the midst of many I will praise Him.

A Psalm of David. The LORD says to my Lord: "Sit at My right hand until I make Your enemies a footstool for Your feet."
verses.note: CHRIST TYPOLOGY / DIVINE COUNCIL: "The LORD (YHWH) says to my Lord ('Adoni): Sit at My right hand until I make Your enemies a footstool for Your feet" — this is the single most-quoted Old Testament verse in the New Testament (Matt. 22:44; 26:64; Mark 12:36; 14:62; 16:19; Luke 20:42-43; 22:69; Acts 2:34-35; Rom. 8:34; 1 Cor. 15:25; Eph. 1:20; Col. 3:1; Heb. 1:3, 13; 8:1; 10:12-13; 12:2). David, the king, calls someone "my Lord" ('Adoni). Jesus uses this to demonstrate that the Messiah is not merely David's descendant but David's Lord — a being of higher ontological status (Matt. 22:41-46). The two "Lords" are YHWH (the Father) and 'Adoni (the Messiah). The Messiah is invited to share God's throne — the highest honor in the universe. "Sit at My right hand" is the position of co-regency and shared authority. No angel is ever offered this seat (Heb. 1:13). The "footstool" imagery derives from ancient Near Eastern conquest ritual where a victorious king placed his foot on the neck of defeated enemies (Josh. 10:24).

verses.flow: The LORD will stretch forth Your strong scepter from Zion, saying, "Rule in the midst of Your enemies."
verses.note: HEBREW: "The LORD will stretch forth Your strong scepter from Zion" (matteh 'uzzekhah yishlach YHWH mitstsiyyon) — Zion is the launching point of messianic rule. The scepter (matteh) fulfills Gen. 49:10: "The scepter shall not depart from Judah... until Shiloh comes." The Messiah rules not in a distant heaven but "in the midst of Your enemies" — present, active, and triumphant.

verses.flow: Your people will volunteer freely in the day of Your power; in holy array, from the womb of the dawn, Your youth are to You as the dew.
verses.note: HEBREW: "Your people will volunteer freely in the day of Your power" ('ammekhah nedavot beyom chelekhah) — nedavot means freewill offerings or willing volunteers. In the Messiah's kingdom, obedience is not coerced but freely given. "From the womb of the dawn" (merechem mishchar) — poetic imagery suggesting the Messiah's origin is from the dawn of creation itself. "Your youth are to You as the dew" (lekhah tal yaldutekha) — the Messiah's followers are innumerable, fresh, and life-giving like morning dew.

verses.flow: The LORD has sworn and will not change His mind, "You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek."
verses.note: CHRIST TYPOLOGY: "The LORD has sworn and will not change His mind: You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek" (nishba' YHWH welo' yinnachem 'attah-kohen le'olam 'al-divrati Malkiy-Tsedeq) — the most theologically significant verse in the psalm alongside v.1. (1) God swears an irrevocable oath — the strongest possible guarantee. (2) The Messiah is declared a priest — but NOT a Levitical priest. (3) "According to the order of Melchizedek" — Melchizedek (Gen. 14:18-20) was king of Salem AND priest of God Most High, predating and superseding the Aaronic priesthood. The author of Hebrews devotes three chapters (5-7) to this verse, arguing that Jesus is a superior high priest: eternal (not subject to death, Heb. 7:23-24), sinless (Heb. 7:26-27), and operating under a better covenant (Heb. 7:22). The union of king and priest in one person was forbidden under the Mosaic law (Uzziah was struck with leprosy for attempting it, 2 Chron. 26:16-21), but Melchizedek held both offices — and so does the Messiah. This is the psalm that establishes Christ as both King (v.1-3) and Priest (v.4) — the two offices united in one eternal person.

verses.flow: The Lord is at Your right hand; He will shatter kings in the day of His wrath.
verses.note: HEBREW: "The Lord ('Adonai) is at Your right hand" — a shift from v.1 where the Messiah sits at YHWH's right hand. Now 'Adonai (which could refer to either YHWH or the Messiah) is at the warrior's right hand, indicating mutual support in battle. The Messiah and God operate in perfect concert.

verses.flow: He will judge among the nations; He will fill them with corpses; He will shatter the chief men over a broad country.

verses.note: HEBREW: "He will drink from the brook by the wayside; therefore He will lift up His head" (minnechal badderekh yishteh 'al-ken yarim ro'sh) — a striking final image. The victorious warrior-priest-king pauses to drink from a stream during His conquest march, then lifts His head in triumph. Some see this as a picture of humiliation followed by exaltation (Phil. 2:7-9) — the Messiah who stooped low (incarnation, cross) and then was raised to the highest place.

Woe is me, for I sojourn in Meshech, for I dwell among the tents of Kedar!
Too long has my soul had its dwelling with those who hate peace.
I am for peace, but when I speak, they are for war.

words.m: verb piel imperfect 3rd masc sing + suffix

My soul waits for the Lord more than the watchmen for the morning; indeed, more than the watchmen for the morning.
O Israel, hope in the LORD; for with the LORD there is lovingkindness, and with Him is abundant redemption.

"Surely I will not enter my house, nor lie on my bed;
I will not give sleep to my eyes, or slumber to my eyelids,

words.m: verb piel imperfect 1st common sing + suffix

There I will cause the horn of David to spring forth; I have prepared a lamp for My anointed.

A Song of Ascents, of David. Behold, how good and how pleasant it is for brothers to dwell together in unity!

They have mouths, but they do not speak; they have eyes, but they do not see;

Those who make them will be like them, yes, everyone who trusts in them.

words.m: verb qal imperfect 1st common sing + suffix

O GOD the Lord, the strength of my salvation, You have covered my head in the day of battle.

verb piel jussive 3rd masc sing + suffix

words.m: verb hiphil imperative 2nd masc sing + suffix
verb hiphil imperative 2nd masc sing + suffix

verses.flow: Let me hear Your lovingkindness in the morning; for I trust in You; teach me the way in which I should walk; for to You I lift up my soul.

words.m: verb hiphil imperative 2nd masc sing + suffix
preposition + verb qal participle active + suffix

verses.flow: Deliver me, O LORD, from my enemies; I take refuge in You.

words.m: verb piel imperative 2nd masc sing + suffix
verb hiphil imperfect 2nd masc sing + suffix

verses.flow: Teach me to do Your will, for You are my God; let Your good Spirit lead me on level ground.

words.m: verb piel imperfect 2nd masc sing + suffix

verses.flow: For the sake of Your name, O LORD, revive me. In Your righteousness bring my soul out of trouble.

words.m: conjunction + verb hiphil sequential perfect 2nd masc sing

verses.flow: And in Your lovingkindness, cut off my enemies and destroy all those who afflict my soul, for I am Your servant.
Of David. Blessed be the LORD, my rock, who trains my hands for war, and my fingers for battle;

words.m: conjunction + verb piel participle active + suffix

verses.flow: my lovingkindness and my fortress, my stronghold and my deliverer, my shield and He in whom I take refuge, who subdues my people under me.

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential imperfect 2nd masc sing + suffix
conjunction + verb piel sequential imperfect 2nd masc sing + suffix

verses.flow: O LORD, what is man, that You take knowledge of him? Or the son of man, that You think of him?
Man is like a mere breath; his days are like a passing shadow.

words.m: conjunction + verb qal imperfect 2nd masc sing

words.g: in/with a mountain or range of hills

words.m: conjunction + verb qal imperfect 3rd masc plur

verses.flow: Bow Your heavens, O LORD, and come down; touch the mountains, that they may smoke.

words.m: conjunction + verb hiphil imperfect 2nd masc sing + suffix
conjunction + verb qal imperfect 2nd masc sing + suffix

verses.flow: Flash forth lightning and scatter them; send out Your arrows and confuse them.

words.m: verb qal imperative 2nd masc sing + suffix
conjunction + verb hiphil imperative 2nd masc sing + suffix

verses.flow: Stretch forth Your hand from on high; rescue me and deliver me out of great waters, out of the hand of aliens,
whose mouth speaks deceit, and whose right hand is a right hand of falsehood.

words.g: in/with + in/with a skin-bag for liquids

verses.flow: I will sing a new song to You, O God; upon a harp of ten strings I will sing praises to You,
who gives salvation to kings, who rescues David His servant from the evil sword.

words.m: verb qal imperative 2nd masc sing + suffix
conjunction + verb hiphil imperative 2nd masc sing + suffix

verses.flow: Rescue me and deliver me out of the hand of aliens, whose mouth speaks deceit, and whose right hand is a right hand of falsehood.
Let our sons in their youth be as grown-up plants, and our daughters as corner pillars fashioned as for a palace;

words.g: a collective name for a flock

verses.flow: let our garners be full, furnishing every kind of produce, and our flocks bring forth thousands and ten thousands in our fields;
let our cattle bear without mishap and without loss, let there be no outcry in our streets!
How blessed are the people who are so situated; how blessed are the people whose God is the LORD!

words.m: verb polel cohortative 1st common sing + suffix
conjunction + verb piel cohortative 1st common sing

verses.flow: A Psalm of Praise, of David. I will extol You, my God, O King, and I will bless Your name forever and ever.

words.m: verb piel cohortative 1st common sing + suffix
conjunction + verb piel cohortative 1st common sing

verses.flow: Every day I will bless You, and I will praise Your name forever and ever.
Great is the LORD, and highly to be praised, and His greatness is unsearchable.
One generation shall praise Your works to another, and shall declare Your mighty acts.
On the glorious splendor of Your majesty and on Your wonderful works, I will meditate.

words.m: verb piel imperfect 1st common sing + suffix

verses.flow: Men shall speak of the power of Your awesome acts, and I will tell of Your greatness.
They shall eagerly utter the memory of Your abundant goodness and will shout joyfully of Your righteousness.
The LORD is gracious and merciful; slow to anger and great in lovingkindness.
The LORD is good to all, and His mercies are over all His works.

words.m: verb hiphil imperfect 3rd masc plur + suffix
verb piel imperfect 3rd masc plur + suffix

verses.flow: All Your works shall give thanks to You, O LORD, and Your godly ones shall bless You.
They shall speak of the glory of Your kingdom and talk of Your power;

words.g: the ruddy i.e. a human being

verses.flow: to make known to the sons of men Your mighty acts and the glory of the majesty of Your kingdom.
Your kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and Your dominion endures throughout all generations.
The LORD sustains all who fall and raises up all who are bowed down.
The eyes of all look to You, and You give them their food in due time.
You open Your hand and satisfy the desire of every living thing.
The LORD is righteous in all His ways and kind in all His deeds.

words.m: verb qal imperfect 3rd masc plur + suffix

verses.flow: The LORD is near to all who call upon Him, to all who call upon Him in truth.

words.m: conjunction + verb hiphil imperfect 3rd masc sing + suffix

verses.flow: He will fulfill the desire of those who fear Him; He will also hear their cry and will save them.
The LORD keeps all who love Him, but all the wicked He will destroy.

words.m: conjunction + verb piel imperfect 3rd masc sing

verses.flow: My mouth will speak the praise of the LORD, and all flesh will bless His holy name forever and ever.
Praise the LORD! Praise the LORD, O my soul!

words.g: to/for gods in the ordinary sense

verses.flow: I will praise the LORD while I live; I will sing praises to my God while I have my being.
Do not trust in princes, in mortal man, in whom there is no salvation.
His spirit departs, he returns to the earth; in that very day his thoughts perish.
How blessed is he whose help is the God of Jacob, whose hope is in the LORD his God,

words.g: the a sea or large body of water

verses.flow: who made heaven and earth, the sea and all that is in them; who keeps faith forever;
who executes justice for the oppressed; who gives food to the hungry. The LORD sets the prisoners free.
The LORD opens the eyes of the blind; the LORD raises up those who are bowed down; the LORD loves the righteous;
the LORD protects the strangers; He supports the fatherless and the widow, but He thwarts the way of the wicked.
The LORD will reign forever, Your God, O Zion, to all generations. Praise the LORD!
Praise the LORD! For it is good to sing praises to our God; for it is pleasant and praise is becoming.
The LORD builds up Jerusalem; He gathers the outcasts of Israel.
He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
He counts the number of the stars; He gives names to all of them.
Great is our Lord and abundant in strength; His understanding is infinite.
The LORD supports the afflicted; He brings down the wicked to the ground.

words.g: to/for gods in the ordinary sense

verses.flow: Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving; sing praises to our God on the lyre,

words.g: a mountain or range of hills

verses.flow: who covers the heavens with clouds, who provides rain for the earth, who makes grass to grow on the mountains.
He gives to the beast its food, and to the young ravens which cry.
He does not delight in the strength of the horse; He does not take pleasure in the legs of a man.
The LORD favors those who fear Him, those who wait for His lovingkindness.
Praise the LORD, O Jerusalem! Praise your God, O Zion!
For He has strengthened the bars of your gates; He has blessed your sons within you.

words.m: verb hiphil imperfect 3rd masc sing + suffix

verses.flow: He makes peace in your borders; He satisfies you with the finest of the wheat.
He sends forth His command to the earth; His word runs very swiftly.
He gives snow like wool; He scatters the frost like ashes.
He casts forth His ice as fragments; who can stand before His cold?

words.m: conjunction + verb hiphil imperfect 3rd masc sing + suffix

verses.flow: He sends forth His word and melts them; He causes His wind to blow and the waters to flow.
He declares His words to Jacob, His statutes and His ordinances to Israel.

words.m: verb qal perfect 3rd common plur + suffix

verses.flow: He has not dealt so with any nation; and as for His ordinances, they have not known them. Praise the LORD!

words.m: verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix

verses.flow: Praise the LORD! Praise the LORD from the heavens; praise Him in the heights!

words.m: verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix
verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix

verses.flow: Praise Him, all His angels; praise Him, all His hosts!

words.m: verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix
verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix

verses.flow: Praise Him, sun and moon; praise Him, all stars of light!

words.m: verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix

verses.flow: Praise Him, highest heavens, and the waters that are above the heavens!

words.m: conjunction + verb niphal perfect 3rd common plur

verses.flow: Let them praise the name of the LORD, for He commanded and they were created.

words.m: conjunction + verb hiphil sequential imperfect 3rd masc sing + suffix

verses.flow: He has also established them forever and ever; He has made a decree which will not pass away.

words.g: in/with a marine or land monster

verses.flow: Praise the LORD from the earth, sea monsters and all deeps;
fire and hail, snow and clouds; stormy wind, fulfilling His word;

words.g: the a mountain or range of hills

verses.flow: mountains and all hills; fruit trees and all cedars;
beasts and all cattle; creeping things and winged fowl;
kings of the earth and all peoples; princes and all judges of the earth;
both young men and virgins; old men and children.
Let them praise the name of the LORD, for His name alone is exalted; His glory is above earth and heaven.

words.m: conjunction + verb hiphil sequential imperfect 3rd masc sing

verses.flow: And He has lifted up a horn for His people, praise for all His godly ones; even for the sons of Israel, a people near to Him. Praise the LORD!
Praise the LORD! Sing to the LORD a new song, and His praise in the congregation of the godly ones.

words.m: preposition + verb qal participle active + suffix

verses.flow: Let Israel be glad in his Maker; let the sons of Zion rejoice in their King.
Let them praise His name with dancing; let them sing praises to Him with timbrel and lyre.
For the LORD takes pleasure in His people; He will beautify the afflicted ones with salvation.
Let the godly ones exult in glory; let them sing for joy on their beds.
Let the high praises of God be in their mouth, and a two-edged sword in their hand,
to execute vengeance on the nations and punishment on the peoples,

words.m: conjunction + verb niphal participle active + suffix

verses.flow: to bind their kings with chains and their nobles with fetters of iron,
to execute on them the judgment written; this is an honor for all His godly ones. Praise the LORD!

words.g: in/with a sacred place or thing

words.m: verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix

verses.flow: Praise the LORD! Praise God in His sanctuary; praise Him in His mighty expanse.

words.m: verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix
verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix

verses.flow: Praise Him for His mighty deeds; praise Him according to His excellent greatness.

words.m: verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix

words.g: in/with a blast of a trumpet

words.m: verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix

words.g: in/with + in/with a skin-bag for liquids

verses.flow: Praise Him with trumpet sound; praise Him with harp and lyre.

words.m: verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix
verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix

verses.flow: Praise Him with timbrel and dancing; praise Him with stringed instruments and pipe.

words.m: verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix
verb piel imperative 2nd masc plur + suffix

verses.flow: Praise Him with loud cymbals; praise Him with resounding cymbals.
Let everything that has breath praise the LORD. Praise the LORD!
